item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This did nothing for me, from the intense Series 1 energy of the article, to the clunky dialogue ("When I was informed of the outcome of that mission… I wasn't the same"), to the Foundation being dick heads for no discernible in-universe reason to the letter that adds no information except, apparently, Eicher's defection to the Chaos Insurgency. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That explains so much
I’ll fix it when I get home lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For reference, "div class="blockquote" is a class defined in the Sigma-9 and Black Highlighter themes: the problem was an extra space here between the words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, thank you so much. And it's alright, I'm just glad that people are enjoying it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope this won't be lost under all the 5000 entries, this is really quite good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'm glad to be back!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not at all, I've had this idea for months, and I haven't read that entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this related to the camera in http://www.scp-wiki.net/5000contestoboebandgeek99 ? They seem to have some similarities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great to see you back, Mew!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made a few edits based on some of your suggestions, and will make more when I get time. Thank you for taking the time to read and share your opinion! I've never been amazing at dialogue, but I'm glad its at least functional.

Also Eicher being an artist is something that's been a part of his character since I created him about 6-7 months ago, so I guess I just didn't do the best job of writing that fact in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! That means a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a very interesting twist. At first, it seemed like this SCP was just going to be a "thing that does something weird" but I'm impressed. The whole tear between the reader and whose side they should be on. The Foundation that wants to protect the world from anomalies or the doctor who wants to help a friend in some unfortunate circumstances. The dialogue seemed organic and not too forced giving a realistic view to the reader. The final reference to the Chaos Insurgency was a nice ending as well.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could use the code I used for the other divs, that's actually what I originally did.
im so bad with coding lol

ill try and fix it aha, thanks ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you just use the same code you used for the other divs, it'd work? Alternatively, if you grabbed it from somewhere, there's probably some CSS for the top of the article to define it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
storm breath just suggested I use that block quote, I don't really know how to fix it ;;

But thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty fun :3

(also btw in Addendum 3, you used "div class="block quote""; but you never defined it so it's not formatted right: every other quote block like that includes in-line formatting so you may want to change it? I would but I'm not sure if the intention is for Addendum 3 to be styled differently?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some things I noticed while reading:

+ Small things
- Small things

SCP-4648 is a Polaroid 600 Camera - One Step Close Up brand Polaroid camera.

Did something garble here? I’d suggest “is a Polaroid 600 OneStep Closeup instant camera”, or other words to that effect.

Sentient/sapient beings will retain the knowledge of their existing counterpart.

The “counterpart” bit was a touch confusing on first read - the article hadn’t established that these are images of specific beings known to the subject. I was thinking more along the lines of generic images of animals, etc. Minor gripe, though.

Canis lupis familiaris (domestic dog) of the German Shepard breed

Should be “German Shepherd”.

following a twenty-four hour day.

“24-hour”?

My artwork— although nowadays are mostly doodles during free time, wasn't the same.

This sentence doesn’t make grammatical sense, though I suppose such sentences could occur in a verbatim transcription of an interview.

So I payed no mind to that. I payed no mind to any of the confusion I felt

payed —> paid

transpired following SCP-4648 Test 10, or, "SCP-4648-1-S."

If SCP-4648-1-S refers to the image of Rodgers, why does it here - and only here - describe the events of the test? Just a small inconsistency.

So you'd rather tear others lives' apart.

others lives’ —> others’ lives

was discovered on the inside off SCP-4648's locker door:

Inside of.

Overall, it’s a simple idea executed nimbly enough without outstaying its welcome. I will say, though, that the dialogue often felt stilted and in-your-face. I felt that Eicher’s hobbyist art and creative block were awkwardly shoehorned into the article to convey his sense of loss. His defection at the end also felt tropey, and I generally wasn’t invested in the friendship between Eicher and Rodgers.

That said, it’s at least functional and I think there’s a time and place for this kind of raw pathos. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: tbh that was a suggested quote by stormbreath and I thought it was cool so I went with it, aha. Anyway, thank you, I'm glad you enjoyed it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure how well the ending quote works for this. The original meaning in Paradise Lost was that basically, the intermittent annoyances of Hell (or the Chaos Insurgency) would attract the attention of the vastly superior forces of Heaven (the Foundation) to attack them.

But otherwise, +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I can't even begin to list everyone who's helped me with this.

This was actually my first greenlit concept when I still needed greenlights, but it got away from me. When I came back from my break of a few months, I wanted to start on some simple stuff, some drafts I've started, so I worked on a tale, and my already started rewrite of the original draft. It has gone sooooooooooooo far from the original garbage original draft, and I have so many wonderful authors to thank for that!

If you helped and you're not listed, I apologize ;; I probably just forgot because seriously I think over twenty people have given me feedback on this

Thanks to:

  • Dyslexion
  • Glewmie
  • DrPillows
  • Simitar
  • BlazingScribe
  • Faceless
  • JakdragonX
  • Jacob Conwell (especially, for helping me figure out a direction)
  • StormBreath
  • Ellie3

And most definitely more.

Until next time!
